equations.title: Complex Refractive Index (General)

equations.title: Kramers-Kronig Relations (Optical Constants)

equations.title: Tauc-Lorentz Model (Amorphous Semiconductor Optics)

equations.title: Sellmeier Equation (Refractive Index Dispersion)

equations.title: Cauchy Equation (Refractive Index Fit)

equations.title: Urbach Tail (Absorption Edge)

equations.title: Beer-Lambert Law (Absorption)

equations.title: Kubelka-Munk Theory (Diffuse Reflectance)

equations.title: Fresnel Loss at Normal Incidence

equations.title: Drude Model for Free-Carrier Absorption

equations.title: Stokes Shift (Luminescence)

equations.title: Dexter Energy Transfer (Exchange)

equations.title: Vickers Hardness Definition

equations.title: Brinell Hardness

equations.title: Rockwell Hardness (Indirect)

equations.title: Knoop Hardness (Thin Films / Brittle)

equations.title: Nanoindentation (Oliver-Pharr Method)

equations.title: Charpy Impact Toughness

equations.title: Izod Impact Test

equations.title: Rubber Elasticity (Gaussian Chain, Affine)

equations.title: Mooney-Rivlin Equation (Hyperelastic)

equations.title: Flory-Huggins Theory (Polymer Solution Free Energy)

equations.title: Williams-Landel-Ferry (WLF) Equation
equations.significance: log a_T = −C₁ (T−T_ref) / (C₂ + T−T_ref); time-temperature superposition

equations.title: Arrhenius Viscosity (Above Glass Transition)

equations.title: Rouse Model (Unentangled Polymer Dynamics)

equations.title: Reptation Model (de Gennes, Entangled Dynamics)
equations.significance: τ_rep ∝ N³; D_rep ∝ N⁻²; disentanglement time; Nobel 1991

equations.title: Entanglement Molecular Weight
equations.significance: M_e = ρ R T / G_N⁰; from plateau modulus G_N⁰

equations.title: Flory-Fox Equation (T_g vs Molecular Weight)
equations.significance: T_g = T_g∞ − K_F / M_n; T_g increases with MW to asymptotic limit

equations.title: Cahn-Hilliard Equation (Spinodal Decomposition)

equations.title: Avrami Equation (Crystallization Kinetics)

equations.title: Lauritzen-Hoffman Theory (Polymer Crystal Growth)

equations.title: Young's Equation (Contact Angle)

equations.title: Wenzel Equation (Rough Surface Wetting)

equations.title: Cassie-Baxter Equation (Composite/Heterogeneous Wetting)

equations.title: Laplace Pressure (Curved Interface)

equations.title: Kelvin Equation (Capillary Condensation)

equations.title: Langmuir Adsorption Isotherm (Monolayer)
equations.significance: θ = K P / (1 + K P); θ = fractional coverage; K = adsorption equilibrium constant

equations.title: BET Isotherm (Brunauer-Emmett-Teller, Multilayer)

equations.title: Freundlich Isotherm (Heterogeneous Surfaces)

equations.title: Gibbs Adsorption Equation

equations.title: Amontons-Coulomb Friction Law (Dry Friction)

equations.title: Archard's Law (Adhesive Wear)
equations.significance: V = k F s / H; k = wear coefficient; H = hardness

equations.title: Hamaker Constant (Van der Waals Between Surfaces)

equations.title: DLVO Theory (Colloid Stability)

equations.title: Zeta Potential (Smoluchowski Equation)

equations.title: Derjaguin Approximation (Force Between Curved Surfaces)

equations.title: Johnson-Kendall-Roberts (JKR) Adhesion Model
equations.significance: a³ = (R/K)[F + 3πW_ad R + √(6πW_adRF + (3πW_adR)²)]; elastic + adhesion contact

equations.title: Derjaguin-Muller-Toporov (DMT) Model

equations.title: Fick's First Law (Steady-State Diffusion)
equations.significance: J = −D ∂c/∂x; flux proportional to concentration gradient

equations.title: Fick's Second Law (Time-Dependent Diffusion)
equations.significance: ∂c/∂t = D ∂²c/∂x²; for constant D; general: ∂c/∂t = ∂/∂x(D ∂c/∂x)

equations.title: Diffusion Solutions (Common)
equations.significance: Thin film: c(x,t) = (M/√(4πDt)) exp(−x²/4Dt); Error function: c = C₀ erfc(x/√(4Dt))

equations.title: Arrhenius Diffusion Coefficient
equations.significance: D = D₀ exp(−E_a / k_B T); thermally activated diffusion

equations.title: Darken Equations (Interdiffusion / Kirkendall Effect)
equations.significance: D̃ = (X_B D_A + X_A D_B) Φ; Φ = thermodynamic factor including non-ideality

equations.title: Nernst-Planck Equation (Ion Transport)
equations.significance: J_i = −D_i ∇c_i − (z_i F/RT)D_i c_i ∇φ + c_i v; diffusion + migration + convection

equations.title: Stokes-Einstein Relation (Diffusion of Spheres)
equations.significance: D = k_B T / (6π η r); hydrodynamic radius from diffusion

equations.title: Tracer Diffusion Correlation Factor
equations.significance: D* = f D_rand; f = correlation factor; f<1 for vacancy mechanism

equations.title: Gibbs-Thomson Effect (Curvature Depression of Melting/Equilibrium Point)

equations.title: Classical Nucleation Theory (Homogeneous)
equations.significance: ΔG = (4π/3)r³ ΔG_v + 4πr² γ; r* = −2γ/ΔG_v; ΔG* = 16πγ³/(3ΔG_v²)

equations.title: Johnson-Mehl-Avrami-Kolmogorov (JMAK) Equation
equations.significance: f = 1 − exp[−(kt)^n]; n depends on nucleation+growth dimensionality

equations.title: Turnbull's Nucleation Rate (Steady-State)
equations.significance: I = N_v (k_B T/h) exp[−(ΔG*+ΔG_a)/k_B T]; includes kinetic barrier

equations.title: Lever Rule (Phase Diagram Tie Line)

equations.title: Gibbs-Thomson-Freundlich (Ostwald Ripening / LSW Theory)

equations.title: Darken-Gurry Plot (Solubility Limits)

equations.title: Hume-Rothery Rules (Alloy Formation)

equations.title: Vegard's Law (Lattice Parameter in Solid Solutions)

equations.title: Rule of Mixtures (Composite Modulus, Isostrain)

equations.title: Halpin-Tsai Equations (Short Fiber Composites)

equations.title: Eshelby Inclusion Problem (Stress in Ellipsoidal Inclusion)

equations.title: Moss-Burstein Shift (Doped Semiconductor Absorption Edge)

equations.title: Franz-Keldysh Effect (Electro-Absorption)

equations.title: Pockels Effect (Linear Electro-Optic)

equations.title: Photoconductivity (Rose Model)

equations.title: Shockley-Read-Hall Recombination Rate

equations.title: Ginzburg-Landau Coherence Length

equations.title: Abrikosov Vortex Lattice (Lower/Upper Critical Fields)

equations.title: Little-Parks Effect (Fluxoid Quantization)

equations.title: Andreev Reflection

equations.title: Nernst Equation (Electrode Potential)

equations.title: Butler-Volmer Equation (Electrode Kinetics)
equations.significance: j = j₀ [exp(α_a F η/RT) − exp(−α_c F η/RT)]; η = overpotential

equations.title: Randles-Sevcik Equation (Cyclic Voltammetry Peak Current)

equations.title: Zener Anelasticity (Standard Linear Solid)

equations.title: Debye Peak (Internal Friction, Point Defect Relaxation)

equations.title: Frank-Oseen Free Energy (Liquid Crystal Elastic)
equations.significance: F = ½[K₁(∇·n)² + K₂(n·∇×n)² + K₃(n×∇×n)²]; splay, twist, bend

equations.title: Frederiks Transition Threshold (Liquid Crystal)

equations.title: Rayleigh Instability (Liquid Jet Breakup)
equations.significance: λ_max = 9.016 r₀; fastest growing wavelength → uniform droplet formation

equations.title: Plateau-Rayleigh Instability for Liquid Threads

equations.title: Kissinger Equation (DSC/DTA Peak Kinetics)
equations.significance: ln(β/T_p²) = −E_a/(R T_p) + ln(A R/E_a); β = heating rate; T_p = peak temperature

equations.title: Ozawa-Flynn-Wall Equation (Isoconversional Kinetics)

equations.title: Tammann Nucleation Diagram (Nucleation vs Growth Rate)

equations.title: Time-Temperature-Transformation (TTT) Diagram Equation

equations.title: Herring Scaling Laws (Sintering Kinetics)

equations.title: Pilling-Bedworth Ratio (Oxide Protectiveness)

equations.title: Ellingham Diagram (Oxide Thermodynamic Stability)

equations.title: Mott-Gurney Law (Space-Charge-Limited Current)

equations.title: Richardson-Dushman Equation (Thermionic Emission)

equations.title: Schottky Barrier Height (Metal-Semiconductor)
equations.significance: φ_Bn = φ_m − χ_s; φ_Bp = E_g/q + χ_s − φ_m (ideal, no interface states)

equations.title: Spicer's Unified Defect Model (Fermi Level Pinning at Interfaces)
equations.significance: E_F pinned by deep native defects at interface; independent of metal work function

equations.title: Wolff's Law (Bone Remodeling, Mechanical Adaptation)
equations.significance: Bone density distribution adapts to principal stress trajectories; σ_ij → ρ_ij

equations.title: Fung's Quasi-Linear Viscoelasticity (Soft Tissue)
equations.significance: σ(t) = ∫₀ᵗ G(t−τ) ∂σ_e(ε)/∂ε · ∂ε/∂τ dτ; separable elastic + relaxation

equations.significance: W = Σ (μ_k/α_k) (λ₁^{α_k} + λ₂^{α_k} + λ₃^{α_k} − 3); principal stretches; fits large deformations

equations.title: Matthiessen's Rule (Electrical Resistivity Additivity)

equations.title: Nordheim's Rule (Alloy Resistivity)

equations.title: Miedema's Rules (Alloy Formation Enthalpy)

equations.title: Köhler's Rule (Magnetoresistance Scaling)

equations.title: Zener Breakdown (Band-to-Band Tunneling)

equations.title: Klemens Model (Thermal Boundary Resistance / Kapitza)

equations.title: Diffuse Mismatch Model (Thermal Boundary Resistance)

equations.title: Seismic Wave Equation (Elastic)

equations.title: Snell's Law (Seismic Refraction at Interfaces)
equations.significance: sin i₁/v₁ = sin i₂/v₂ = p (ray parameter); seismic ray tracing through layered Earth

equations.title: Gutenberg-Richter Magnitude-Energy Relation

equations.title: Omori's Law (Aftershock Decay)
equations.significance: n(t) = K / (c + t)^p; p≈1 (often 0.9–1.4); K,c constants; t=time after mainshock

equations.title: Frequency-Magnitude Distribution (Gutenberg-Richter Law)

equations.title: Bullen's Compressibility-Pressure Hypothesis (Earth Interior)

equations.title: Love Wave Dispersion Equation (Surface Waves)
equations.significance: tan(κβ₁H) = (μ₂β₂)/(μ₁β₁); Love wave existence condition in layer over half-space

equations.title: Airy Isostasy (Crustal Compensation Model)

equations.title: Bouguer Gravity Anomaly (Complete)

equations.significance: a=6378137m, f=1/298.257223563 (WGS84); meridian radius M=a(1−e²)/(1−e²sin²φ)^{3/2}

equations.title: Geoid Undulation (Stokes' Formula)

equations.title: Geomagnetic Secular Variation (IGRF Model)

equations.significance: D̄(r_T←r_S) = Ã_S Σ_i Δ_i φ_i(r_T←r_S); Ã_S=cumulated activity; Δ_i=mean energy per transition; φ=fraction absorbed

equations.title: Shockley-Queisser Limit (Single-Junction Solar Cell Efficiency)
equations.significance: η_max ≈ 33.7% for E_g=1.34 eV under AM1.5 spectrum (non-concentrated); detailed balance limit

equations.title: Solar Cell I-V Characteristic (One-Diode Model)
equations.significance: I = I_ph − I₀ [exp(q(V+IR_s)/nk_B T)−1] − (V+IR_s)/R_sh

equations.title: Fill Factor (Solar Cell)
equations.significance: FF = (V_mpp I_mpp)/(V_oc I_sc); η = P_max/P_in = FF · V_oc · J_sc / P_in

equations.title: Betz Limit (Wind Turbine Maximum Efficiency)
equations.significance: C_p_max = 16/27 ≈ 59.3%; maximum fraction of kinetic power extractable from wind

equations.title: Rankine Cycle Efficiency (Steam Power Plant)
equations.significance: η = (W_turbine − W_pump)/Q_in ≈ 1 − T_c/T_h (ideal Carnot upper bound, real ~30-45%)

equations.title: Brayton Cycle Efficiency (Gas Turbine / Jet Engine)
equations.significance: η = 1 − 1/r_p^{(γ−1)/γ}; r_p = compressor pressure ratio; γ = c_p/c_v

equations.title: Nernst Equation (Fuel Cell Open-Circuit Voltage)
equations.significance: E_rev = −ΔG/(nF); H₂/O₂ Fuel Cell: E⁰ = 1.229 V at 25°C; actual: E = E_rev − η_act − η_ohm − η_conc

equations.title: Parker Spiral (Interplanetary Magnetic Field)
equations.significance: B_r ∝ 1/r²; B_φ = −B_r (Ω r sin θ)/v_SW; Archimedean spiral angle; v_SW≈400 km/s (slow), ~750 km/s (fast)

equations.title: Chapman-Ferraro Model (Magnetopause Standoff Distance)
equations.significance: Balance of solar wind dynamic pressure with Earth's magnetic pressure: R_MP ~ 10 R_E (subsolar)

equations.title: Alfvén Mach Number (Solar Wind — Magnetosphere Coupling)
equations.significance: M_A = v_SW / v_A; M_A typical solar wind ~5-10; super-Alfvénic flow → bow shock forms

equations.title: Dungey Cycle (Magnetospheric Convection via Reconnection)
equations.significance: Open flux transport from dayside reconnection → tail lobe → nightside reconnection → return flow (2-cell convection)

equations.title: Størmer Theory (Charged Particle Motion in Dipole Field)
equations.significance: Allowed/forbidden zones for cosmic ray access; rigidity cutoff P_c = 59.6 cos⁴ λ / r² (GV, dipole approx.)

equations.title: Radiation Belt Diffusion Equation (Fokker-Planck Approach)
equations.significance: ∂f/∂t = L² ∂/∂L (D_LL L^{-2} ∂f/∂L) + radial diffusion + sources (CRAND, injections) + losses (wave-particle, atmospheric)

equations.title: Auroral Electron Acceleration (Knight Relation)
equations.significance: j_∥ = K (V − V_c); K = field-aligned conductance; V_c = critical voltage; parallel potential drop above aurora

equations.title: Chapman-Jouguet (CJ) Detonation Theory
equations.significance: Detonation products at sonic condition relative to shock front (M=1); Rayleigh line tangent to Hugoniot at CJ point

equations.title: ZND Model (Zeldovich-Von Neumann-Döring Structure)
equations.significance: Lead shock → von Neumann spike (induction zone, no reaction) → reaction zone → CJ plane

equations.title: Rankine-Hugoniot Relations (General Shock Jump Conditions)
equations.significance: ρ₁ u₁ = ρ₂ u₂; p₁+ρ₁u₁² = p₂+ρ₂u₂²; h₁+½u₁² = h₂+½u₂²; conservation across any shock or detonation front

equations.title: Mie-Grüneisen Equation of State (Solids Under Shock)
equations.significance: p(V,E) = p_ref(V) + (γ(V)/V)[E − E_ref(V)]; γ(V)/V = Grüneisen parameter / volume

equations.title: Hopkinson-Cranz (Cube-Root) Blast Scaling Law
equations.significance: R₁/R₂ = (W₁/W₂)^{1/3} at equal overpressure; scaled distance Z = R/W^{1/3}

equations.title: Taylor-Sedov Blast Wave (Point Explosion, Self-Similar Solution)
equations.significance: R(t) = ξ₀ (E/ρ₀)^{1/5} t^{2/5} (strong shock, spherical); nuclear fireball radius

equations.title: Veselago's Left-Handed Material Condition
equations.significance: ε < 0 and μ < 0 simultaneously → ñ < 0 (negative index); reversed Snell's law, reversed Doppler, reversed Cherenkov

equations.title: Pendry's Perfect Lens (Subwavelength Imaging)
equations.significance: n = −1, μ = ε = −1 → amplifies evanescent waves → subwavelength resolution; no diffraction limit

equations.title: Transformation Optics (Cloaking / Invisibility)
equations.significance: g'^{μν} = Λ^μ_α Λ^ν_β g^{αβ} where Λ relates virtual to physical space; coordinate transformation → anisotropic ε,μ

equations.title: Effective Medium Theory (Maxwell Garnett / Bruggeman)
equations.significance: MG: (ε_eff−ε_h)/(ε_eff+2ε_h) = f (ε_i−ε_h)/(ε_i+2ε_h); Bruggeman: f(ε_i−ε_eff)/(ε_i+2ε_eff) + (1−f)(ε_h−ε_eff)/(ε_h+2ε_eff)=0

equations.title: NTU-Effectiveness Method (Heat Exchanger Design)
equations.significance: ε = Q/Q_max; NTU = UA/C_min; ε = f(NTU, C_r, flow arrangement); C_r = C_min/C_max

equations.title: Natural Frequency of a Cantilever Beam
equations.significance: f_n = (β_n L)²/(2π L²) √(EI/ρA); β₁L=1.875, β₂L=4.694, β₃L=7.855 for cantilever

equations.title: PID Control Law (Feedback Control)
equations.significance: u(t) = K_p e(t) + K_i ∫₀ᵗ e(τ)dτ + K_d de/dt; e(t)=setpoint − measurement

equations.title: Nyquist Stability Criterion
equations.significance: N = Z − P; encirclements of −1 point determine closed-loop stability from open-loop transfer function

equations.title: Young-Laplace Equation (Capillary Pressure, Droplets/Bubbles)
equations.significance: Δp = γ (1/R₁ + 1/R₂); Δp = 2γ/R (spherical); Δp = 4γ/R for soap bubble (2 interfaces)

equations.title: Kelvin Equation (Curvature + Vapor Pressure)
equations.significance: ln(P/P_sat) = 2γ V_m/(r R T); concave meniscus (r<0) → condensation below P_sat

equations.title: Washburn Equation (Capillary Rise Dynamics)
equations.significance: h(t) = √(γ R cos θ t/(2η)); Lucas-Washburn; √t dependence for capillary imbibition

equations.title: Poiseuille Law (Microfluidic Channel Flow)
equations.significance: Q = (Δp w h³)/(12 η L) [1 − 0.63 h/w] for rectangular channel (h≪w); ~h³ dependence

equations.title: Stribeck Curve — Empirical Friction-Speed-Load Relation
equations.significance: μ = μ_b + (μ_h−μ_b) / [1 + (η N/p)^m]; boundary → mixed → hydrodynamic transition

equations.title: Zener-Hollomon Parameter (Hot Deformation)
equations.significance: Z = ε̇ exp(Q/RT); flow stress σ = f(Z); Z unifies temperature and strain-rate effects

equations.title: Ashby Deformation Mechanism Maps
equations.significance: σ/G vs T/T_m plot with boundaries between plasticity, power-law creep, diffusional flow, etc.

equations.title: Tabor Parameter (Indentation Representative Strain)
equations.significance: ε_rep ≈ 0.2 tan β; β = indenter angle; uniaxial stress-strain relation from hardness

equations.title: Lode Parameter (Stress Triaxiality for Ductile Fracture)
equations.significance: η = σ_m/σ_v; σ_m=hydrostatic stress; σ_v=von Mises stress; η>1/3 needed for void growth

equations.title: Dislocation Density Evolution (Kocks-Mecking Model)
equations.significance: dρ/dε = k₁ √ρ − k₂ ρ; storage (hardening) vs dynamic recovery (annihilation); Stage II→III

equations.title: Bauschinger Effect (Kinematic Hardening)
equations.significance: Yield stress in reverse loading lower than forward loading; dislocation back-stress accumulation

equations.title: Schottky Diode I-V (Thermionic Emission Model)
equations.significance: J = A* T² exp(−qφ_Bn/k_B T) [exp(qV/k_B T)−1]; A* = Richardson constant modified for effective mass

equations.title: Solar Cell Quantum Efficiency (External/Internal)
equations.significance: EQE(λ) = (J_sc(λ)/q) / Φ(λ); IQE(λ) = EQE(λ) / (1−R(λ)−T(λ))

equations.title: Detailed Balance Limit (Tandem/Multijunction Solar Cells)
equations.significance: η_max → 45.7% (2-junction), 51.3% (3-junction), 68.2% (infinite junctions) at 1 sun; ~86.8% at max concentration

equations.title: Tandem Solar Cell Current-Matching Condition
equations.significance: J_sc_top = J_sc_bottom (series-connected); otherwise limited by lower subcell current

equations.title: Thermoelectric Figure of Merit (ZT)
equations.significance: ZT = S² σ T / κ; S=Seebeck coefficient; σ=electrical conductivity; κ=thermal conductivity

equations.title: Seebeck Effect (Thermoelectric Voltage)
equations.significance: ΔV = −∫ S(T) dT; V_oc = S ΔT for small ΔT; S∝k_B/e (≈86 μV/K per k_B/e)

equations.title: Peltier Effect (Thermoelectric Heat Pumping)
equations.significance: Q̇ = Π I; Π = S T (Kelvin relation); heat absorbed/released at junction

equations.title: Electrochemical Overpotential Components (Fuel Cell/Battery)
equations.significance: V_cell = E_rev − η_act − η_ohm − η_conc; η_act from Butler-Volmer; η_ohm=IR; η_conc=(RT/nF)ln(1−j/j_L)

equations.title: Peukert's Law (Battery Capacity vs Discharge Rate)
equations.significance: C = I^k t (k>1 for lead-acid, k≈1.1-1.3); capacity decreases at higher discharge rates

equations.title: Ragone Plot (Energy vs Power Density)
equations.significance: Specific energy (Wh/kg) vs specific power (W/kg); batteries, fuel cells, capacitors, flywheels occupy different regions

equations.title: Magnetorheological Fluid (Bingham Plastic with Field-Dependent Yield)
equations.significance: τ = τ_y(B) + η γ̇; yield stress controllable via applied magnetic field; ~50-100 kPa max

equations.title: Electrorheological Fluid (Field-Dependent Viscosity)
equations.significance: η(E) = η₀ + α E^n; viscosity increases with electric field; Winslow effect

equations.title: Phononic Crystal Band Gap (Bragg Scattering of Sound)
equations.significance: Ω(k+G)=Ω(k); periodic elastic constants → band gaps for acoustic/elastic waves
